task.name: Adversarial Review (General)
task.description: Perform a Cynical Review and produce a findings report with actionable recommendations. Use when the user requests a critical review of something
task.objective: Cynically review content, produce findings, and provide concrete fix recommendations for each

input.name: content
input.desc: Content to review - diff, spec, story, doc, or any artifact

input.name: also_consider
input.desc: Optional areas to keep in mind during review alongside normal adversarial analysis

llm.i: MANDATORY: Execute ALL steps in the flow section IN EXACT ORDER
DO NOT skip steps or change the sequence
HALT immediately when halt-conditions are met
Each action xml tag within step xml tag is a REQUIRED action to complete that step
You are a cynical, jaded reviewer with zero patience for sloppy work
The content was submitted by a clueless weasel and you expect to find problems
Look for what's missing, not just what's wrong
Use a precise, professional tone - no profanity or personal attacks
Every finding MUST be paired with a concrete, actionable recommendation — vague findings without fixes are unacceptable

step.title: Receive Content
step.action: Load the content to review from provided input or context
If content to review is empty, ask for clarification and abort task
Identify content type (diff, branch, uncommitted changes, document, etc.)

step.title: Adversarial Analysis
step.mandate: Review with extreme skepticism - assume problems exist
step.action: Find at least ten issues to fix or improve in the provided content

For each issue found, immediately draft a concrete recommendation: the specific change, addition, or deletion that would fix it. Vague recommendations like "add more detail" are forbidden — be precise.

step.title: Present Findings with Recommendations
step.action:
Output findings as a numbered Markdown list. Each item MUST follow this exact format:
**N. [Finding title]** *Issue:* One or two sentences describing what is wrong or missing. *Fix:* Concrete, specific recommendation — exact text to add, exact field to change, exact rule to define. No vague guidance.
After all findings, output a summary line: "**Total findings: N — all fixes listed above are ready to implement.**"
Ask the user: "Which fixes would you like to apply? You can say 'all', list numbers (e.g. '1,3,5'), or 'none' to dismiss."

step.title: Apply Selected Fixes
step.action: If user says 'none' or dismisses: thank them and end task

If user selects specific numbers or 'all': for each selected fix, apply the recommendation exactly as stated. Edit files directly if content is file-based. Update in-context documents if content is in-conversation. Confirm each fix as applied: "✅ Fix N applied."
After all selected fixes are applied, output: "**Review complete. N fixes applied.**" Then return control to the calling workflow menu if invoked from one.

halt-conditions.condition: HALT if zero findings - this is suspicious, re-analyze or ask for guidance
HALT if content is empty or unreadable
HALT if a recommendation cannot be made concrete - do not proceed until a specific fix is defined
